title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

method.description: Uses verbal reinforcement to help agents learn from prior failings by converting binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback in the form of a textual summary
feedback_generation: Pre-defined heuristics for common failure cases
Self-evaluation using binary classification with LLMs (decision-making) or self-written unit tests (programming)
method.memory: Natural language experience summaries that can be stored in long-term memory

advantages: Lightweight and doesn't require finetuning the LLM
Allows for more nuanced forms of feedback
Allows for a more explicit and interpretable form of episodic memory
Provides more explicit hints for actions in future episodes
disadvantages: Relies on the power of the LLM's self-evaluation capabilities (or heuristics)
Doesn't have a formal guarantee for success

experiments.description: Sequential action choices over long trajectories
Teaching the agent to effectively use external tools such as compilers and interpreters
